executive_summary.overview: UPS engaged Anglepoint for a comprehensive IBM software asset management assessment covering up to 250 IBM products across 70,000 servers, 270,000 workstations, 5 physical mainframes, and 10,000 Red Hat servers. The engagement identified $8.9M in total optimization opportunities through license compliance analysis and cost savings recommendations.
key_accomplishments: Achieved 100% cost avoidance on $1.08M identified compliance risk across 8 IBM products including InfoSphere DataStage, WebSphere Application Server, and DB2 products
Identified $3.59M in sub-capacity cost savings opportunities across 23 products with more licenses than in use
Identified $2.56M in non-ILMT cost savings opportunities across 43 products with excess licenses
Completed comprehensive Effective License Position analysis for 115 IBM distributed products with excess licensing

key_metrics.label: Total Optimization Identified
key_metrics.context: Combined cost avoidance and savings potential over 1-year period

key_metrics.label: Sub-Capacity Products with Excess Licenses
key_metrics.context: Products found to have more licenses than currently in use

key_metrics.label: Non-ILMT Products with Excess Licenses
key_metrics.context: Products with more licenses than deployment requires

key_metrics.label: Products with Zero Deployment
key_metrics.context: 17 sub-capacity and 32 non-ILMT products carrying active S&S with no deployments

recommendations: Remove 23 IBM sub-capacity products with excess licenses from upcoming renewal if no growth plans exist - potential savings $2.34M
Validate and remove 17 sub-capacity products with active S&S but zero deployments - potential savings $2.91M
Remove 43 non-ILMT products with excess licenses from upcoming renewal - potential savings $1.82M
Confirm and remove 32 non-ILMT products with active S&S but zero deployments - potential savings $742K
Implement cost savings recommendations by June 30, 2025 target date
primary_risks: IBM InfoSphere DataStage Linux for System z over-licensing exposure of $339,517
IBM WebSphere Application Server Network Deployment compliance gap of $303,635
IBM DB2 Enterprise Server Edition deployment increased by 3,520 units (Q3 2024 to Q2 2025) representing $107,043 additional exposure
ELA Renewal deadline June 30, 2025 requires immediate license quantity decisions
IASP ELP Delivery to IBM due July 31, 2025 for all sub-capacity products
market_risks: IBM Cloud Pak for Business Automation showing only 36.59% utilization with $767,981 savings opportunity
IBM Storage Suite for IBM Cloud Paks showing 0% utilization requiring deployment validation
Multiple products with 0% utilization indicate potential shelf-ware requiring business justification
additional_insights: IBM ODM Server deployment decreased significantly from 23,400 to 7,420 units representing $858,446 in positive monetary change
10 products showed decreased deployment over past 4 quarters representing optimization progress
6 products showed increased deployment requiring ongoing monitoring for compliance
Products with utilization over 60% not recommended for renewal reduction per best practice guidelines
next_steps: Implement cost savings recommendations targeting June 30, 2025 completion
Schedule WatsonX Implementation working with product stakeholders
Begin Non-PVU data collection to establish annual baseline
Issue IASP ELP to IBM by July 31, 2025 with Anglepoint copied
Determine license renewal quantities for June 30, 2025 ELA renewal
